revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Inarimori Kofun]][[ja:稲荷森古墳]][[zh:稻荷森古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Nan'yō, Tōhoku, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Inarimori Kofun | native_name =稲荷森古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Inari-mori Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Inarimori Kofun | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Inarimori Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.039639|N|140.157361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nan'yō, Yamagata|en|Nan'yō, Yamagata|qid=Q850496|lt=Nan'yō, Yamagata|1=Nan'yō, Yamagata}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = burial tumulus | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access =yes (archaeological park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Inarimori Kofun '''|稲荷森古墳|Inarimori kofun}} is the name of an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|qid=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} containing a large [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Nan'yō, Yamagata|en|Nan'yō, Yamagata|qid=Q850496|lt=Nan'yō, Yamagata|1=Nan'yō, Yamagata}} in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201086|title=稲荷森古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== Discovered around 1933, it has been speculated that the Inarimori tumulus was a ''kofun'' from 1938; however, as a preliminary excavation in 1961 found only {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} shards, this hypothesis was discredited. The area also contained the remains of a village from around the middleof the [[Kofun period]]. When the mound was cleared in 1977 to 1979, its keyhole-shape was discovered, and more detailed investigation from 1977 to 1978 confirmed that it indeed dates from the latter-half of the 4th century AD. Further investigations were performed by the Nan'yō Board of Education from 1987-88, but the interior of the tomb has never been investigated and no [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has yet been discovered.
The Inarimori kofun has an overall length of 96 meters, making it the largest in Yamagata, and the 7th largest in the Tōhoku region. It was made by partially cutting away of an isolated hill on the right bank of the Yoshino River on the northern edge of the Yonezawa basin. The tomb is orientated to face south-southwest. The circular portion has a diameter of 62 meters and height of 9.2 meters and is constructed in three tiers. The rectangular portion has a width of 30 meters and height of five meters and is constructed in one tier. No ''[[fukiishi]]'', ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' or trace of a moat have been discovered, although shards of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery and some human bones were found.
The ''kofun'' and surrounding 10,182.7 hectares are maintained as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]]. It is located approximately five minutes by car from the {{ill|Akayu Station|en|Akayu Station|qid=Q1137103|lt=Akayu Station|1=Akayu Station}} on the {{ill|Yamagata Shinkansen|en|Yamagata Shinkansen|qid=Q252048|lt=Yamagata Shinkansen|1=Yamagata Shinkansen}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamagata)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamagata)|qid=Q9633915|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamagata)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamagata)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== [[Category:Inari-mori Kofun}} *[http://yamagatakanko.com/spotdetail/?data_id=830 Enjoy Yamagata home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://nanyo-bunka.jp/cultural_retrieval/index.php/detail?no=1 Nan'yo home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.pref.yamagata.jp/cgi-bin/yamagata-takara/?m=detail&id=1127 Cultural Assets of Yamagata] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
